idea.hook: How big is our Solar System compared to a nebula?
facts: Our Solar System, including the vast Oort Cloud, spans about 2 light-years across.
The iconic Orion Nebula, a stellar nursery, is approximately 24 light-years in diameter.
Imagine placing Solar Systems side-by-side across the nebula...
idea.payoff: ...you could fit 12 of them end-to-end across the Orion Nebula's width!
idea.title: Nebula: Solar Systems Deep! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Solar System vs. Nebula: How Big?
scenes.visual: Split screen: left side a detailed shot of our Solar System (planets orbiting the sun), right side a vibrant image of a nebula.

scenes.text: Our Solar System (incl. Oort Cloud) is ~2 light-years across.
scenes.visual: Animation zooming out from a central star (Sun) to reveal an immense, faint sphere representing the Oort Cloud, against a field of stars.

scenes.text: The Orion Nebula is ~24 light-years in diameter.
scenes.visual: A stunning, full-screen, vibrant image of the Orion Nebula, slowly panning across its swirling gas and dust.

scenes.text: Imagine placing Solar Systems end-to-end across the nebula...
scenes.visual: The Orion Nebula fills the background. A small, simple icon representing a Solar System appears on one side, then slowly implies a series of duplicates extending across the screen.

scenes.text: ...you could fit 12 Solar Systems across the Orion Nebula!
scenes.visual: The Orion Nebula fills the background. Twelve distinct, scaled icons representing Solar Systems appear aligned horizontally across the entire width of the screen, emphasizing the sheer scale.

script.thumbnail_text: Nebula vs. Solar System: The Shocking Scale!
